h2h_summary: Panama and Croatia have never previously met in a senior international match, making their 2026 World Cup clash on June 23 a historic first encounter.
key_players_home: Jose Fajardo (striker, top scorer in qualifying)
key_players_away: Luka Modric (central midfielder, captain, AC Milan, 6th World Cup)
team_style_home: Panama play an organised 3-4-3/5-4-1 system under Thomas Christiansen, relying on defensive compactness, set-piece threat, and quick vertical counter-attacks with wing-backs Murillo and Davis providing width.
team_style_away: Croatia under Zlatko Dalic favour a 4-3-3 or 3-4-2-1 structure built around midfield control and possession, with Modric pulling strings as a creative No.10 and ball-playing defenders initiating from the back.
wc_history_home: Panama made their historic debut at the 2018 World Cup in Russia, finishing last in their group with three defeats, though Felipe Baloy scored their first-ever World Cup goal in a 6-1 loss to England.
wc_history_away: Croatia are one of the most successful small nations in World Cup history, finishing third in their debut in 1998, reaching the final in 2018 (losing 4-2 to France), and claiming another bronze medal in 2022.
group_context: Group L is widely expected to be topped by England, with Croatia and Ghana fighting for the second qualification spot and Panama considered heavy underdogs; Panama must beat Croatia to have any realistic chance of advancing, while Croatia need a win to put pressure on England.
strengths_home: Defensive organisation and hard-to-break compact shape
Set-piece danger, particularly from corners and free-kicks
Counter-attacking pace through Fajardo and Waterman up front
strengths_away: World-class midfield quality led by Modric and Kovacic
Vast World Cup experience and big-game temperament
Kramaric's clinical finishing and Gvardiol's defensive solidity
